briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_init() is called with a valid node-ID
Then the device is initialized with the default values and the requested node-ID and ERRNUM_SUCCESS error number is set calls rbtree_init()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_init() is called with 0xff
Then the device is initialized with the default values and the requested node-ID and ERRNUM_SUCCESS error number is set calls rbtree_init()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_init() is called with zero
Then a null pointer is returned and ERRNUM_INVAL error number is set calls set_errnum()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_init() is called with an invalid node-ID
Then a null pointer is returned and ERRNUM_INVAL error number is set calls set_errnum()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an initialized device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_fini() is called
Then the device is finalized calls rbtree_foreach() calls co_obj_destroy() if !LELY_NO_CO_OBJ_NAME: calls free()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_netid() is called with a valid network-ID
Then 0 is returned, the network-ID is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_netid() is called with an network-ID of 0xff
Then 0 is returned, the network-ID is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_netid() is called with an invalid network-ID
Then -1 is returned, the network-ID is not set calls set_errnum()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with a valid node-ID
Then 0 is returned, requested node-ID is set calls co_obj_set_id()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with a node-ID
Then 0 is returned, requested node-ID is set, the sub-object values of the form $NODEID { "+" number } are updated calls co_obj_set_id()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary, the entry is of a basic data type

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with a valid node-ID
Then 0 is returned and the requested node-ID is set, the sub-object values of the form $NODEID { "+" number } are updated calls co_obj_set_id()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary, the entry is of a non-basic data type

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with a valid node-ID
Then 0 is returned and the requested node-ID is set, the sub-object values are not modified calls co_obj_set_id()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with a node-ID of 0xff
Then 0 is returned and the required node-ID is set calls co_obj_set_id()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with a node-ID equal zero
Then -1 is returned and the required node-ID is not set calls set_errnum()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_id() is called with an invalid node-ID
Then -1 is returned and the required node-ID is not set calls set_errnum()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_idx() is called with a maximum number of indices to return and a memory area to store the object indices
Then 0 is returned and the memory area is not modified calls rbtree_first() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls rbtree_size()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_idx() is called with no memory area to store the results
Then 0 is returned calls rbtree_size()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing one object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_idx() is called with no memory area to store the results
Then 1 is returned calls rbtree_size()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing one object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_idx() is called with a memory area to store the results
Then 1 is returned, the memory area contains the index of the object calls rbtree_first() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls rbnode_next() calls rbtree_size()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing many objects in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_idx() is called with a maximum number of indices to return equal to the number of the objects in the dictionary, a memory area to store the results
Then a number of objects in the object dictionary is returned, the memory area contains indices of these objects calls rbtree_first() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls rbnode_next() calls rbtree_size()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing many objects in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_idx() is called with maximum number of indices to return lower than the number of the objects in the dictionary, a memory area to store the results

Then a number of the contained objects is returned, requested number of indices in order from lowest to highest are stored in the memory area calls rbtree_first() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls rbnode_next() calls rbtree_size()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_insert_obj() is called with a pointer to an object (co_obj_t)
Then 0 is returned, the object is inserted into the object dictionary calls rbtree_find() calls rbtree_insert()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_insert_obj() is called with a pointer to the object (co_obj_t) which was inserted into other device's object dictionary
Then -1 is returned, the object is still inserted into other device's object dictionary and is not inserted into target device's object dictionary

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_insert_obj() is called with a pointer to the object (co_obj_t) which was already inserted into the object dictionary
Then 0 is returned, the object is still inserted into the object dictionary

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_insert_obj() is called with a pointer to the another object (co_obj_t) with an index equal to the index of the object which was already inserted into the other device's object dictionary
Then -1 is returned, the initial object is still inserted into the other device's object dictionary

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_remove_obj() is called with a pointer to the object (co_obj_t)
Then 0 is returned, the object is not present in the device calls rbtree_remove() calls rbnode_init()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_remove_obj() is called with a pointer to an object not inserted into the dictionary
Then -1 is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_find_obj() is called with the object's index
Then a pointer to the object is returned calls rbtree_find()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_find_obj() is called with an index of a non-existing object
Then a null pointer is returned calls rbtree_find()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_find_obj() is called with an index
Then a null pointer is returned calls rbtree_find()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object with a sub-object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_find_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index of the sub-object
Then a pointer to the sub-object is returned calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_find_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_find_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index
Then a null pointer is returned calls co_dev_find_obj()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_find_sub() is called with an index of the object and a sub-index
Then a null pointer is returned calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_find_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing three objects in the object dictionary, object with the lowest index is inserted first

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_first_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object with a minimal index is returned calls rbtree_first()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing three objects in the object dictionary, object with the lowest index is inserted between objects with higher indices

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_first_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object with a minimal index is returned calls rbtree_first()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing three objects in the object dictionary, object with the lowest index is inserted last

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_first_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object with a minimal index is returned calls rbtree_first()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing one object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_first_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object is returned calls rbtree_first()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_first_obj() is called
Then a null pointer is returned calls rbtree_first()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing three objects in the object dictionary, object with the highest index is inserted first

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_last_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object with a maximum index is returned calls rbtree_last()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing three objects in the object dictionary, object with the highest index is inserted between objects with lower indices

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_last_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object with a maximum index is returned calls rbtree_last()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing three objects in the object dictionary, object with the highest index is inserted last

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_last_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object with a maximum index is returned calls rbtree_last()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing one object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_last_obj() is called
Then a pointer to the object is returned calls rbtree_last()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_last_obj() is called
Then a null pointer is returned calls rbtree_last()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_vendor_id() is called with a vendor ID
Then the requested vendor ID is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_product_code() is called with a number
Then the requested product code is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_revision() is called with a number
Then the requested revision is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_baud() is called with supported bit rates
Then the requested bit rates are set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_rate() is called with a baudrate value
Then the requested baudrate is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_lss() is called with true.
Then the LSS support flag is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_dummy() is called with supported data types for mapping dummy entries in PDOs
Then the data types supported for mapping dummy entries in PDOs are set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_val() is called with an index and a sub-index of the entry
Then a pointer to the entry's value is returned calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a null pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_val() is called with any index and sub-index
Then a null pointer is returned calls co_sub_get_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_val() is called with an index and a sub-index of a non-existing entry
Then a null pointer is returned calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_val() is called with an index and sub-index of the entry, a pointer to a value to be copied, a number of bytes present at the pointer
Then the length of the value is returned, the requested value is set calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_set_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with an empty object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_val() is called with an index and a sub-index of a non-existing entry, a pointer to a value to be copied, a number of bytes present at the pointer
Then 0 is returned and ERRNUM_INVAL error number is set calls co_dev_find_sub() calls set_errnum()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_val_<typename>() is called with an index, a sub-index of the entry and a value
Then the requested value is set and can be obtained using co_dev_get_val_<typename>()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with pointers to memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer

Then a number of the bytes read from the buffer is returned, the index and the sub-index are stored in the memory and the value read from the buffer is set as the entry value calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_get_type() if LELY_NO_MALLOC: calls co_type_is_array() calls co_val_init() calls co_sub_set_val() calls co_val_addressof() calls co_val_sizeof() if !LELY_NO_MALLOC: calls co_val_fini()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with no memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer

Then the number of bytes read from the buffer is returned, the value read from the buffer is set as the entry value calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() if LELY_NO_MALLOC: calls co_type_is_array() calls co_val_init() calls co_val_read() calls co_sub_set_val() calls co_val_addressof() calls co_val_sizeof() if !LELY_NO_MALLOC: calls co_val_fini()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with null pointers to the memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then a number of the bytes read from the buffer is returned, nothing is changed calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_find_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with null pointers to the memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a null pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with null pointers to the memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a null pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with null pointers to the memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer, the buffer is too small
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with null pointers to the memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer, the buffer does not contain the whole value
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed calls co_val_read()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an object of the array type in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with a memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer

Then a number of the bytes read from the buffer is returned, the index and the sub-index are stored in the memory area, the value read from the buffer is set as the entry value calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_type_is_array() calls co_type_is_array() calls co_val_init_array() calls co_sub_set_val() calls co_val_addressof() calls co_val_sizeof()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_sub() is called with null pointers to the memory area to store an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer with a value in the concise DCF format and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer, the buffer contains additional byte for a value

Then a number of the bytes read from the buffer is returned, the value is not changed calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_type_is_array() if LELY_NO_MALLOC: calls co_type_is_array() calls co_val_init()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer

Then a number of the bytes written to the buffer is returned, the buffer contains the expected data in the concise DCF format calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_sub_get_val() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an empty object in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_sub() is called with the index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then a number of the bytes that would have been written to the buffer had the buffer been sufficiently large is returned, the buffer remains empty calls co_dev_find_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary, the entry is of a domain type

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer

Then a number of the bytes written to the buffer is returned, the buffer contains the expected data in the concise DCF format calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_sub_get_val() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index and null buffer pointers

Then the number of bytes that would have been written had the buffer been provided is returned, nothing is changed calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_sub_get_val() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of a buffer and a null pointer

Then a number of the bytes written to the buffer is returned, the buffer contains the expected data in the concise DCF format calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_sub_get_val() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_sub() is called with an index and a sub-index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer, the buffer is too small

Then a number of the bytes that would have been written to the buffer had the buffer been sufficiently large is returned, the buffer remains empty calls co_dev_find_sub() calls co_sub_get_type() calls co_sub_get_val() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t), a buffer containing a value in DCF format

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_dcf() is called with pointers to the memory area to store the minimum object index and the maximum object index, a pointer to a start of the buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then a number of bytes read from the buffer is returned, the requested entry, the minimum and the maximum indices are set to the requested values calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_read_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t), a buffer containing a value in DCF format

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_dcf() is called with no memory area to store the minimum object index and the maximum object index, a pointer to a start of the buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then a number of bytes read from the buffer is returned, the requested entry is set to the requested value calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_read_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t), a zeroed buffer

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_dcf() is called with no memory area to store the minimum object index and the maximum object index, a pointer to the zeroed buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then a number of bytes read from the buffer is returned, nothing is changed calls co_val_read()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t), a buffer containing an incomplete value in DCF format

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_read_dcf() is called with no memory area to store the minimum object index and the maximum object index, a pointer to a start of the buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed calls co_val_read() calls co_dev_read_sub()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_dcf() is called with a minimum and a maximum object index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer

Then a number of bytes written to the buffer is returned, the buffer contains the expected data in the concise DCF format calls co_dev_first_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_first_sub() calls co_sub_get_subidx() calls co_dev_write_sub() calls co_sub_next() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_dcf() is called with a minimum and a maximum object index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer, minimum object index is greater than the index of the contained object

Then a number of bytes that would have been written to the buffer had the buffer been sufficiently large is returned, the buffer remains empty calls co_dev_first_obj() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_dcf() is called with a minimum and a maximum object index, a pointer to a start of an empty buffer and a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer, maximum object index is lower than the index of the contained object

Then a number of bytes that would have been written to the buffer had the buffer been sufficiently large is returned, the buffer remains empty calls co_dev_first_obj() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_val_write()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) containing an entry in the object dictionary

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_write_dcf() is called with a minimum and a maximum object index and null buffer pointers

Then a number of bytes that would have been written had the buffer been sufficiently large is returned, nothing is changed calls co_dev_first_obj() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_first_sub() calls co_sub_get_subidx() calls co_dev_write_sub() calls co_obj_next()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_get_tpdo_event_ind() is called with no memory area to store the results
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_set_tpdo_event_ind() is called with a pointer to a TPDO event indication function and a pointer to a user-specified data
Then the requested TPDO event indication function is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with an index and a sub-index of a non-existing sub-object
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with disabled PDO mapping

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping and does not contain any TPDO Communication Parameter objects (0x1800-0x19ff)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping and a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with the Highest Sub-index Supported sub-object (0x00) outside allowed value range

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping and a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with an invalid COB-ID of the TPDO

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping and a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with a reserved TPDO transmission type

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping and a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with acyclic TPDO transmission type but does not contain the matching TPDO Mapping Parameter object (0x1a00)

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping, a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with acyclic TPDO transmission type and the matching TPDO Mapping Parameter object (0x1a00) with a mapping using a different object index

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping, a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with acyclic TPDO transmission type and the matching TPDO Mapping Parameter object (0x1a00) with a mapping using a different sub-index

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was not called calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with no custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping, a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with acyclic TPDO transmission type and the matching TPDO Mapping Parameter object (0x1a00) with the entry mapped

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then nothing is changed calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping, a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with acyclic TPDO transmission type and the matching TPDO Mapping Parameter object (0x1a00) with the entry mapped

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was called once calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping, a TPDO Communication Parameter object (0x1800) with an event-driven TPDO transmission type and the matching TPDO Mapping Parameter object (0x1a00) with the entry mapped

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry
Then the indication function was called once calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a device (co_dev_t) with a custom TPDO event indication function set, the object dictionary contains an entry with enabled PDO mapping, multiple TPDO Communication Parameter objects (0x1800-0x19ff) with acyclic TPDO transmission types and matching TPDO Mapping Parameter objects (0x1a00-0x1bff), the entry is mapped in at least one of them

detaileddescription.para: When co_dev_tpdo_event() is called with the index and sub-index of the entry

Then the indication function was called once for every matched TPDO in order of increasing PDO numbers calls co_dev_chk_tpdo() calls co_dev_find_obj() calls co_obj_next() calls co_obj_get_idx() calls co_obj_addressof_val()

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When initializing co_id using CO_ID_INIT macro
Then the initialized structure is filled with the correct default values

memberdef.name: LELY_CO_DEFINE_TYPE
memberdef.initializer: { \ dev_holder.reset(new CoDevTHolder(0x01u)); \ dev = dev_holder->Get(); \ \ CoObjTHolder obj_holder(0x1234u); \ CoSubTHolder sub_holder(0xabu, CO_DEFTYPE_##a); \ co_sub_t* const sub = obj_holder.InsertSub(sub_holder); \ CHECK(sub != nullptr); \ co_obj_t* const obj = obj_holder.Take(); \ CHECK_EQUAL(co_type_sizeof(CO_DEFTYPE_##a), \ co_sub_set_val_##c(sub, 0x42 + co_dev_get_id(dev))); \ co_sub_set_flags(sub, CO_OBJ_FLAGS_VAL_NODEID); \ CHECK_EQUAL(0, co_dev_insert_obj(dev, obj)); \ const co_unsigned8_t new_id = 0x14; \ \ const auto ret = co_dev_set_id(dev, new_id); \ \ CHECK_EQUAL(0, ret); \ CHECK_EQUAL(new_id, co_dev_get_id(dev)); \ const co_obj_t* const out_obj = co_dev_first_obj(dev); \ CHECK_EQUAL(static_cast<co_##b##_t>(0x42 + new_id), \ *static_cast<const co_##b##_t*>( \ co_sub_get_val(co_obj_first_sub(out_obj)))); \ }

memberdef.name: LELY_CO_DEFINE_TYPE
memberdef.initializer: { \ dev_holder.reset(new CoDevTHolder(0x01u)); \ dev = dev_holder->Get(); \ \ CoObjTHolder obj(0x1234u); \ CoSubTHolder sub(0xabu, CO_DEFTYPE_##a); \ CHECK(obj.InsertSub(sub) != nullptr); \ CHECK_EQUAL(0, co_dev_insert_obj(dev, obj.Take())); \ \ const auto set_ret = co_dev_set_val_##c(dev, 0x1234u, 0xabu, 0x42u); \ \ CHECK_EQUAL(co_type_sizeof(CO_DEFTYPE_##a), set_ret); \ \ const co_##b##_t get_ret = co_dev_get_val_##c(dev, 0x1234u, 0xabu); \ \ CHECK_EQUAL(get_ret, static_cast<co_##b##_t>(0x42u)); \ }

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint_least8_t *const buf1, const uint_least8_t *const buf2, const size_t n)
memberdef.name: CheckBuffers

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.
